_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 10 years of experience
clinical_indications: Irreparable loss of the blood supply
Malignancy: high- and low-grade malignant bone and soft-tissue tumours

preauth.label: Clinical notes justifying the diagnosis and indication for proceeding with surgery along with planned line of treatment
X-ray/MRI/PET labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) -affected limb
Clinical photograph of the affected part showing the gangrene/injury/severe anatomical deformity

cpd_claim.q: Does the Post-op clinical photograph show the amputation/Disarticulation?
The detailed procedure notes mention the reason for Disarticulation/ Amputation are submitted?

common_queries: Post-operative clinical photograph does not clearly show amputation/disarticulation.
Detailed procedure notes do not mention the reason for disarticulation/amputation.
Clinical notes justifying diagnosis and indication for surgery with planned line of treatment are missing or incomplete.
X-ray/MRI/PET of affected limb is missing, not labelled with patient ID/date, or incorrectly labelled for side (Left/Right).
Clinical photograph of the affected part is missing or does not clearly show gangrene/injury/severe anatomical deformity.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) are missing or incomplete.
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes are missing or incomplete.

clinical_key_pointers: The forequarter amputation also called as Inter scapula thoracic amputation involves the surgical removal of one entire upper extremity and shoulder gridle, including scapula and a portion of the clavicle

Hindquarter amputation is synonymous with hemipelvectomy, disarticulation of the innominate bone, inter-ilio-abdominal amputation, sacroiliac disarticulation, trans iliac amputation, inter pelvi-abdominal amputation and trans pelvic amputation.
Shoulder disarticulation and forequarter amputation are usually related to malignancy or severe trauma.
